releaseNotes: Adds the Premium Mobile Craft lane: the invisible details that make a launched app feel premium rather than flashy, baked into the boilerplate. New references/premium-mobile-craft.md encodes the doctrine (premium = the compounding effect of fifty invisible decisions, not wild animation or heavy blur) across the five details — press states & spring physics, subtle/purposeful motion in the 150-300ms band, haptics discipline (confirm decisions/state changes only, never navigation/scroll/idle taps), keyboard behavior (input/submit never covered, intentional dismissal), and loading/empty states (size-preserving skeleton/shimmer over bare spinners; empty states that explain what/why + one action). Platform routing makes SwiftUI/latest Swift the primary target (with iOS 26 Liquid Glass affordances for chrome) and gives React Native (pressto/react-native-ease/react-native-pulsar/react-native-keyboard-controller) and Flutter parity, all driven from the shared DesignTokens.Motion scale and honoring Reduce Motion.

Ships templates/design-system/PremiumCraft.swift as drop-in SwiftUI boilerplate: a PremiumPressStyle button style (spring press feedback + optional confirming haptic), PremiumMotion springs derived from the tokenized motion scale, a .premiumAnimation(_:value:) modifier that collapses under Reduce Motion, a semantic Haptics layer (declarative .sensoryFeedback(for:trigger:) plus imperative one-shots), keyboard helpers (interactive drag-to-dismiss, tap/swipe focus-blur, a Done key-row), and a ShimmerModifier/.skeleton(isLoading)/EmptyStateView for loading and empty states. The file imports no framer-motion (check:template-safety stays green) and consumes the generated DesignTokens.Motion contract rather than hardcoding durations.

Wires the lane into SKILL.md routing (new When-To-Load rule) and design-visual-system.md (routing step 6a), extends templates/ux-patterns/UX_PATTERNS.md with a Premium Craft Details table, bug traps, and an acceptance-checklist item (kept the check:ux-patterns required phrases intact), and registers the authoritative Apple HIG/SwiftUI source URLs (Playing haptics, Motion, Loading, Applying Liquid Glass to custom views) in source-registry.yaml.

Adds native iOS proof awareness for Codex Desktop and CLI users. references/xcodebuildmcp-testing.md is now the native iOS proof hub: Codex Desktop/XcodeBuildMCP tools are first-class when exposed (session_show_defaults before build/run/test, build_run_sim/test/screenshot/log proof recorded), SnapshotPreviews is supported for preview-only XCTest PNG/JSON exports through TEST_RUNNER_SNAPSHOTS_EXPORT_DIR, and serve-sim is supported for browser-visible booted-simulator streaming/control at routes such as http://localhost:3200. The guidance explicitly separates simulator/preview proof from backend/provider proof and Apple signing/distribution readiness.

Audit hardening: check-native-ios-proof no longer accepts SnapshotPreviews as the raw real-app screenshot capture route by itself. Ready iOS screenshot packets must name a real app, simulator, or device capture route such as MobAI, Codex Desktop/XcodeBuildMCP, serve-sim, on-device capture, or real app capture, and any SnapshotPreviews support evidence must state its preview-only limitation. Validator fixtures cover both SnapshotPreviews-only screenshots and missing SnapshotPreviews limitations.

Ships check-native-ios-proof plus a native-ios-proof-route-skipped LaunchBench scenario and failure card. The validator checks iOS readiness claims for a named native proof route, Codex Desktop/XcodeBuildMCP tool evidence, SnapshotPreviews export paths and preview-only limits, serve-sim URL/booted-simulator evidence and limits, provider-proof pairing, and the App Store signing/distribution limitation.

Threads the new native iOS proof standard through generated business templates and maintainer docs: PRODUCTION_READINESS.md gains a Native iOS Proof matrix, SCREENSHOTS.md and CONTENT_ASSETS.md recognize Codex Desktop/XcodeBuildMCP/SnapshotPreviews/serve-sim inputs, PROJECT_STATE.yaml tracks the three tool routes, AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md role templates tell future agents how to use them, and source-registry.yaml tracks getsentry/SnapshotPreviews, EmergeTools/SnapshotPreviews, and EvanBacon/serve-sim for weekly freshness.

Adds the Claude-vs-Codex runtime split and Claude Dynamic Workflows lane for the pre-build stages. New references/dynamic-workflows.md documents, for both agents and humans, when to use a Dynamic Workflow over inline/subagents/Compound Engineering, the ultracode and /effort ultracode and /deep-research triggers (Claude Code v2.1.154+ research preview), the agent()/parallel()/pipeline() API with per-agent model and worktree/remote isolation, the runtime limits (16 concurrent / 1000 total agents, no mid-run input, in-session resume), the six patterns mapped to each pre-build stage, token discipline (/goal, /loop, budgets), the quarantine pattern for untrusted reviews/social/scraped input, and save/ship-as-skill — with Claude owning research-through-spec and Codex owning the core app build.

Wires the lane into SKILL.md (new Claude vs Codex Routing And Dynamic Workflows contract section, a When-To-Load rule, and an Operating Posture bullet), cross-links parallel-agent-orchestration.md (Subagents Vs Dynamic Workflows note plus the workflows doc in its source basis), and threads the runtime split into the generated business-repo templates/repo-agent-entrypoints/AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md so launched repos keep the pre-build-on-Claude / build-on-Codex discipline. Registers the Claude Code workflows docs and the Anthropic launch writeup in source-registry.yaml.

Frames the Claude-vs-Codex split as a recommendation and default bias, not a hard requirement: the skill never blocks, gates, or refuses work in whichever runtime is in front of it. Adds a Detect Codex And Recommend Claude For Pre-Build behavior in dynamic-workflows.md (runtime-detection signals plus a recommend-once-never-block protocol) so Codex users on pre-build stages get the Claude+Dynamic-Workflows tip surfaced once, recorded in PROJECT_STATE.yaml, then the work continues in the current runtime with the same fan-out/adversarial-verification/quarantine shapes. Softens the wording across SKILL.md, README.md, and the business-repo AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md templates accordingly, and extends the runtime_split state contract with current_runtime/recommended_*/handled_by/recommendation_made fields.

Verifies the existing RevenueCat State of Subscription Apps 2026 figures in the monetization/onboarding anti-pattern digest against the source SOSA 2026 PDF (all cited numbers — 10.7% vs 2.1% hard-vs-freemium D35, $2.32 vs $0.27 D14 RPI, $35.89 vs $6.67 / $62.19 vs $10.69 price-tier LTV, 4.6% reach $10K with ~75% drop-off from $1K, the 25.5/37.4/42.5% trial-length curve, and the Day-0 cancellation figures — confirmed accurate) and extends the digest with three findings the report carried but the skill had not captured.

revenue-monetization.md gains §8a Billing Health And Involuntary Churn (Google Play ~31% of cancellations are involuntary billing failures vs ~14% on the App Store; recovery system = grace period/account hold, RevenueCat billing-issue webhook -> dunning push/in-app banner/payment.failed email, one-tap update-payment, and split voluntary-vs-involuntary churn analytics), an AI-retention evidence bullet in §4c (~41% higher revenue per payer but ~30% faster churn), and two new §10 anti-patterns (#13 treat involuntary churn as inevitable; #14 bank on AI novelty for retention). onboarding-conversion.md Plan And Trial Mix gains the paid-intro-offer-as-free-trial-alternative finding (only ~9.3% of apps use promo offers; ~30% of new subs enter via intro discount; hobby apps over-rely at 65-99% vs top performers 0-10%). source-registry.yaml records the 2026-06-05 verification. No new external source URLs introduced; figures framed as defaults to TEST, with pricing/plan/trial/paywall-model changes still founder-gated.

Adds three further SOSA 2026 findings verified against the source PDF: (1) revenue-monetization.md §4c gains the first-renewal-inflection evidence bullet (~half of monthly and ~3-in-4 yearly subs don't pass renewal #1; +18-30pp jump to renewal #2; yearly retains ~3-10x weekly; Y1 retention softening annual ~31%->28% / monthly ~10%->8%; win by accelerating time-to-value before renewal #1, not by gating harder); (2) new §8b Reactivation And Win-Back (reactivation is plan-dependent: monthly ~18-24% / weekly ~7-10% / annual ~4-6% with annual churn largely permanent; reactivation fires when the problem recurs not when the email lands; offer pause-instead-of-cancel and one-tap return; plan-duration-segmented reactivation analytics); (3) §6 gains the web-revenue-adoption signal (~41% of top-tier apps have web revenue vs ~1.3% hobby, ~31x gap; web ~3.2% of revenue globally) plus web-funnel design notes (sell the problem before the solution; discounted paid trial outperforms free trial on web; run web vs app-install creative separately). No new external source URLs.

Expands the Launch Narrative & Cadence lane with the agency's full operational 'X Launch Playbook & Checklist' (sourced from the shared doc): references/launch-narrative-cadence.md gains the six-phase checklist (Foundation bold claim/outcome-over-feature/network/activatable communities/roadmap-as-content; Outlier Research with the 500+/50+/50+ ~10x heuristic; Content Creation write-every-post-yourself, format-matching, launch-video rules, and a defensible rage-bait line gated by ethics-guardrail.md; Amplification Roster budget/vetting bands; the Launch-Day Timing Engine three gates; and the Follow-Up Window), the Sourcing-vs-Ranking algorithm model, a Launch-Killing Mistakes list, Final Principles, and the agency's stated success bands (~2-10M views / 500-2,000+ comments / 1,000-5,000+ retweets) framed as aim-at targets, not promises.

Turns templates/growth/LAUNCH_NARRATIVE.md into a real six-phase checklist artifact and extends check:launch-narrative to require the new spine (Bold Claim, Outcome Over Feature, Marketing At The Table, Outlier Research, Amplification Roster, Launch-Day Timing Engine, Follow-Up Window, Sourcing And Ranking) on top of the existing copy guardrails. Keeps claims hygiene: the agency's headline aggregates (100M+ views, $1M+ execution, 2-3x reply uplift) are marked agency-stated and must not be repeated as the launching company's own results; no new external source URLs are introduced.

Adds a research-first localization lane: localization is now a market-selection decision made from search-demand evidence, not a translate-everything task. New references/localization-market-research.md defines the Demand -> Prioritize -> Localize -> Measure method, maps the 'keyword popularity > X / low difficulty / localization priority per country' research onto the skill's existing stack (AppKittie keyword popularity/difficulty, Apple Search Ads, App Store Connect App Analytics, XPOZ) with no TryAstro/MCP dependency, and ranks markets into Tier 1 full-localize / Tier 2 metadata-only / Tier 3 defer that drive effort across every surface (store metadata/keywords/screenshots, paywall/offers, landing+hreflang/llms.txt, lifecycle email, and paid storefronts).

Ships templates/localization-market-research/ (LOCALIZATION_MARKET_RESEARCH.md market opportunity matrix + localization-market-research.html board), wires the lane into SKILL.md Phase 1 research/ASO routing/load-rules/Deliverable Standard, tracks it as research-lane evidence in PROJECT_STATE.yaml, and cross-links aso-store-ops.md, app-store-listing-prep.md, paid-user-acquisition.md, surfaces-b2c.md, change-cascade.md, geo-seo.md, and launch-coverage.md.

Adds the check-localization-research validator (translate-first guard plus required priority tiers, per-storefront popularity/difficulty signals, and demand-research tool sourcing), wires it into run-validator-fixtures (pass + translate-first fail cases), and adds the localization-translate-first-without-demand-research LaunchBench scenario so the translate-first miss is regression-covered, not prose-only.

Adds the Launch Narrative & Cadence lane: a public-launch playbook that treats every release as an event. references/launch-narrative-cadence.md encodes the tentpole-vs-weekly-heartbeat model, algorithm compounding / standing audience, marketing-at-the-table roadmap influence, the launch-day run-of-show (post order and who replies to what), and feeling-first copy craft. It is grounded in verified launch artifacts (Koji by Brilliant ~4.71M impressions, Moda ~4.50M, ChatGPT 'Paperwork' ~2.66M) and the Shown Media agency's stated method, with explicit caveats marking the MrBeast-alumni, 10B-views, CEO-Emmy, and 'many startups died' claims as unverified and not to be repeated.

Ships growth/LAUNCH_NARRATIVE.md as the per-business artifact and a deterministic check:launch-narrative validator enforcing the 2026 copy guardrails (no hashtags, no emojis, no link in the main announcement post) against fenced post copy, plus required sections, a named emotional angle, and traceability refs. Wires the lane into SKILL.md routing, the growth lane evidence in templates/PROJECT_STATE.yaml, the root/runtime audit chains, README validator list, source-registry.yaml (Shown Media, the verified launch posts, Brilliant Koji), and a launch-narrative-feeling-first-missing LaunchBench scenario.

Adds a monetization-and-growth anti-pattern digest so agents know the cozy defaults NOT to reach for, grounded in the RevenueCat State of Subscription Apps 2026 report (no new source URLs; reuses the registered state-of-subscription-apps entry). revenue-monetization.md gains a new §10 digest index plus detailed traps (assume you are the exception; soft paywall by reflex; push monthly/skip annual; price low to be 'humble'); onboarding-conversion.md gains Conversion Anti-Patterns (short-trial reflex; ignore Day 0 and bank on a day-30 win-back; one experiment a year); paid-user-acquisition.md gains Anti-Patterns (run ads before you can read the result; optimize creative before the paywall; chase vanity views); app-store-listing-prep.md frames localization as a high-leverage conversion lever (not optional translation) and cross-links the existing App Preview lever; viral-growth-loops.md cross-links the vanity-views trap. Each benchmark is framed as a default to TEST, not dogma, and pricing/plan/trial/paywall-model changes stay founder-gated.

Adds the monetization-cozy-default-stack-unexamined LaunchBench scenario (validated by check-onboarding-conversion) and a launchbench-evals.md catalog entry so the default-stack miss is regression-covered, not prose-only.

Public-readiness pass: adds an MIT LICENSE and license metadata, removes hardcoded author machine paths/username from AGENTS.md, references/skill-versioning.md, and check-skill-version.ts (now uses the B2C_SKILL_SOURCE env override with a repo-relative fallback), and makes validate:skill skip gracefully when the maintainer-only skill-creator linter is not installed so a fresh clone can run npm run audit end to end.

Adds a README "Who this is for & what you need" section that states the subscription-app scope, the Claude Code/Codex runtime requirement, and discloses that third-party provider accounts are optional and swappable; registers the Claude Code product URL in source-registry.yaml.

Adds the Business Control workspace read-model schema plus a deterministic renderer/checker that adapts state/business.json and PROJECT_STATE.yaml into state/workspace.generated.json, validated by check:business-control-plane-workspace.

Wires the Business Control workspace adapter into root/runtime package scripts, audit gates, README/AGENTS/control-plane docs, and prototype query-param loading so JSON-backed local experiments stay reproducible.

Adds validator-backed Session Continuity: generated business AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/APP_AGENTS.md/orchestrator prompts now force resume/status/handoff work to reconstruct state from PROJECT_STATE.yaml, launch-cockpit.html, ORCHESTRATION.md, PRODUCTION_READINESS.md, FAILURE_CARDS.md, and git status before choosing work. Adds continuity state fields, ORCHESTRATION.md continuity handoff slots, check-continuity-contract, validator fixtures, LaunchBench coverage, and an agent-behavior eval for continuity drift.

Post-commit audit hardening: restores source-root maintainer parity for 11-star/emotional-design checks, adds package/version parity enforcement, makes emotional_design a required state lane, tightens emotional-design board and live-claim proof validation, makes Design Room versioning render/check before commit, requires exact template evidence paths, makes iOS App Preview 1 founder-deferral explicit, wires BRAND.md and DEMO_VIDEO.md into starter artifacts and agent entrypoints, refreshes runtime-install documentation, and removes stale command/reference drift.

In-depth audit pass (5-dimension review): hardens check-emotional-design.ts (derives scarcity/social-proof attestation from parsed card blocks instead of stripped text, fixing a false positive; implements the previously-undocumented spend-prompt-near-reward scan; extends dark-pattern phrase scans to ONBOARDING/SPEC/APP_STORE_LISTING; expands confirmshaming/commitment-guilt patterns; reads EMOTIONAL_DESIGN.md in the children scan and makes under-13 an error). Fixes check-store-screenshots.ts (unreachable board-missing check now gated to done; count regex matches 'N screenshots').

Reconciles documentation to the real validator: ethics-guardrail.md §3/§5 now list the actually-enforced attestation fields and the emotional_design.* rule codes (the old check-ethics-guardrail.ts codes were stale); adds EU AI Act Art. 5 and California AADC to the regulatory landscape; canonicalizes all PostHog event names (perceived_effort_*, peak_moment_reached, intent_mirror_continued, commitment_made); repoints the 12 card Spec Pointers to experience-cards.md; corrects Schultz (Science 1997) and IKEA-effect (Journal of Consumer Psychology) citations; fixes the emotional-design.html curve so it renders (concrete stub coordinates), axis label, and headings.

Elevates the App Store app preview video to a first-class growth lever (verified against Apple docs: up to 3 previews precede screenshots, the first autoplays muted as a ~5-second silent hook with a poster frame that must sell on its own). SCREENSHOTS.md now requires an App Preview Video (Autoplay Hook) section and an Asset Knowledge Brief so both screenshots and the preview are engineered from RESEARCH.md (user/problem), 11_STAR_EXPERIENCE.md (magical moment), and EMOTIONAL_DESIGN.md (Emotional North Star + Experience Card), and are always produced through the screenshot ASO skill (ParthJadhav/app-store-screenshots / ios-screenshots / app-preview-video). check-store-screenshots enforces this and it is auditable for existing apps; adds the app-preview-hook-missing-or-generic failure card and the app-preview-video-hook-missing LaunchBench eval.

Wires the Emotional Experience System into the downstream artifact templates: ANALYTICS.md (emotion-card event catalog + Dark-Pattern Watch), DESIGN.md (three-level tone + per-card motion/reduced-motion spec), TECH_SPEC.md (Experience Card frontstage/backstage + perceived-effort step-to-operation map), PRODUCTION_READINESS.md (per-card bright-line evidence rows), and ONBOARDING.md (card-timing contract).

Restructures EMOTIONAL_AUDIT.md per-journey tables from a 12-column block into a readable 8-column table plus a Findings & Pathway sub-block per journey, adds per-journey Emotional Curve markers for all 10 journeys, caps step scores at 7-star, corrects the lane key to lanes.emotional_design, and strengthens the acceptance gate to cover Critical and High journeys.

Adds the Emotional Experience System lane: an emotionally-intelligent consumer-product design layer grounded in behavioral science (Skinner, Schultz, Eyal, Fogg, Cialdini, Buell-Norton, Kahneman) with a 12-card Experience Card deck led by the four named cards — Commitment, Variable Reward, Perceived Effort Delay, and Intent Mirroring.

Ships a Producer path (charge a feature with engineered emotion → EMOTIONAL_DESIGN.md + emotional-design.html) and an Auditor path (walk every user journey on-device, score it through a six-lens review, and return a pathway to a better state → EMOTIONAL_AUDIT.md).

Adds the check:emotional-design validator enforcing the artifact contract plus a deterministic dark-pattern guardrail: high-risk cards (variable reward, streak, scarcity, urgency, social proof) require an ethics attestation, a user-control escape hatch, a counter-metric, and a mechanism-specific truthfulness proof; live copy is scanned for fake scarcity, fabricated social proof, confirmshaming, and commitment guilt.

Wires the lane into SKILL.md routing, launch-coverage, PROJECT_STATE.yaml (lanes.emotional_design), failure cards, and LaunchBench, and threads the cards through the 11-star 6/7-star levels, onboarding-conversion timing, and analytics-attribution events.
